plumage.cap: Solid black crown from bill to nape

alarm_call_metrics.ordering_rule: Alphabetical (A before B before C before D); invariant
alarm_call_metrics.optional_constituency: Any note type can be omitted
alarm_call_metrics.d_note_encoding: Inversely correlated with predator size; more D = higher threat

examples: Four discrete note types (A, B, C, D) combined by ordering rules
Any element optional; ordering invariant (always alphabetical)
D-note count variable — encodes predator threat level (Templeton et al. 2005, Science)
Parallels chord voicing: discrete elements + combination rules = open-ended meaning
theory_nodes.music_theory_parallel: Combinatorial structure — finite elements combined by rules to produce open-ended meaningful output. The defining property of both language (Hockett 1960) and music theory (chords from notes, phrases from motifs).

examples: More D notes = smaller, more agile predator = higher threat to chickadee-sized birds
Fewer D notes = larger, slower predator = lower threat
Experimentally verified: taxidermic mount presentations produce reproducible D-note counts
Playback of high-D-note calls elicits stronger mobbing response
theory_nodes.music_theory_parallel: Dynamics as encoded information — D-note count functions as a dynamic marking (intensity encoded through repetition count, not amplitude). More sophisticated than simple volume variation.

examples: Descending minor third (~6:5 frequency ratio) between fee and bee notes
Most common interval in infant-directed speech across cultures (Fernald 1989)
Default interval in children's taunts and calls worldwide (Brandt, Gebrian & Slevc 2012)
Most frequent interval in simplest musical systems globally (Nettl 1983)
Convergent acoustic solution — bird and human arrive at same basin of attraction
theory_nodes.music_theory_parallel: The minor third as cognitive universal — the interval that requires minimal neural processing to produce and perceive. A physical constant of vocal production, not a cultural invention.

examples: Alarm call broadcast on shared acoustic channel — open protocol, multiple receivers
Red-breasted Nuthatches decode D-note count (Templeton & Greene 2007, PNAS)
Pacific Wrens reduce singing on elevated-threat alarms
Chickadee as nuclear species in mixed-species flocks — hub of information network
Audio bus analogy: one source, shared medium, species-specific decoding chains
theory_nodes.music_theory_parallel: Broadcast bus — signal from one source routed to multiple receivers simultaneously. Each receiver processes with its own channel strip. The mixer/bus of the forest acoustic network.

examples: D-note count = communicative intensity level (not volume)
More repetitions = higher urgency = stronger mobbing response
Piano/forte as information in music — dynamic markings encode structural and emotional meaning
Chickadee encodes through count, not decibels — more sophisticated dynamic system
theory_nodes.music_theory_parallel: Dynamic encoding through structural repetition rather than amplitude variation. Information in the pattern, not the loudness.

examples: The bird's English name IS a transcription of its call: 'chick-a-dee'
Sound becoming language becoming taxonomy — the name is the sound
One of few birds whose common name is an onomatopoeic rendering of its primary vocalization
The ambassador species: most PNW residents learn this bird by name AND sound simultaneously
theory_nodes.music_theory_parallel: Sound as identity — the call IS the name. In music: a leitmotif that is so recognizable it becomes the character's name (as Wagner's motifs became shorthand for the characters they represent).

examples: Degrees 0-2: physics of sound (silence, FM, AM) — how sound is produced
Degree 3: meaning in sound (referential syntax) — what sound means
The transition from bioacoustics to biosemiotics
Largest structural leap in the first four degrees — crosses from sound domain to language domain
theory_nodes.music_theory_parallel: The transition from sound design to composition — from the physics of timbres and waveforms to the arrangement of meaningful structures. The same leap a DAW user makes from synthesis to songwriting.

cross_references.title: Wings of the Pacific Northwest
cross_references.connection: Passerine taxonomy and distribution; this release adds combinatorial syntax, alarm broadcast, and music-theory mapping

cross_references.title: Living Systems of the PNW
cross_references.connection: Mixed woodland ecology, mixed-species flock dynamics, food caching and spatial memory

cross_references.title: Bio-Physics Sensing
cross_references.connection: Information encoding — D-note count as measured variable encoding predator threat. Biological sensor with encoded output signal.

cross_references.title: Smart Home Electronics
cross_references.connection: Biological sensor network parallel: detect event → encode info → broadcast to receivers. Same architecture, biological implementation.

cross_references.title: Seattle 360 Music
cross_references.connection: Part A parallel: Ernestine Anderson at degree 3 — both are communicators who stayed while others moved. Resident voices whose constancy defines the community.

cross_references.title: Great Blue Heron (Degree 0)
cross_references.connection: Non-referential silence at degree 0 → referential syntax at degree 3. From absence of meaning to encoded meaning in three degrees.

cross_references.title: Pacific Wren (Degree 1)
cross_references.connection: Degree 1 eavesdrops on degree 3: wrens reduce singing on chickadee alarm. First cross-degree information dependency in the circle.

cross_references.title: Varied Thrush (Degree 2)
cross_references.connection: Direct predecessor. AM sustain (territorial presence, no referent) → combinatorial syntax (specific, decoded threat information). Sound-as-presence to sound-as-language.

part_a_parallel.shared_principle: Both are communicators who stayed while others moved. Anderson stayed in Seattle when jazz moved to New York. The chickadee stays year-round when other species migrate. Both are resident voices whose constancy defines the community.
resonance_points: Resident communicator: Anderson's voice defined Seattle jazz; the chickadee's alarm defines the forest's information network — both stay, both broadcast, both are infrastructure
Clear, named, immediately recognizable: Anderson's voice was unmistakable; the chickadee's call is the most immediately identifiable bird sound in the PNW — 'chick-a-dee' IS its name

Community anchor: Anderson held the Seattle jazz community together through decades; the chickadee is the nuclear species that holds mixed-species flocks together — remove either and the community degrades

Meaning in delivery: Anderson communicated emotion through phrasing and dynamics, not pyrotechnics; the chickadee communicates threat level through D-note count, not volume — both encode meaning in structure

degree_arc.progression: Silence (0°) → cascade complexity (1°) → sustained tone (2°) → referential syntax (3°)
degree_arc.key_insight: Degree 3 breaks out of the acoustic-physics domain that defined degrees 0-2. The circle has moved from HOW sound is produced to WHAT sound MEANS. This is the largest structural leap so far — not a quantitative increase in acoustic complexity, but a qualitative shift into the domain of language and meaning.

synthesis_types.degree_3: Combinatorial (discrete elements + ordering rules + variable repetition)

register_progression.degree_3: Alto-soprano (2-5+ kHz, multiple note types spanning range)

content_progression.degree_1: Identity + fitness (who is singing, how well)
content_progression.degree_3: Referential threat information (what is there, how dangerous is it)

cognition.hippocampal_neurogenesis: Seasonal neuron production for spatial memory of food cache locations (Barnea & Nottebohm 1994)
cognition.brain_plasticity: Hippocampus expands in autumn (caching season), contracts in spring
cognition.implication: Combinatorial syntax emerges from a cognitively complex organism — not simple reflexive signaling

conservation.state_status: Common, stable, well-distributed across all woodland habitats with woody cover
primary_threats: West Nile virus (documented mortality in some populations)
Habitat fragmentation reducing mixed-species flock connectivity
conservation.ecosystem_note: Chickadee abundance is infrastructure — mixed-species flock early-warning system depends on chickadees being common. Decline would cascade through flock community.

key_sites.name: Seattle urban parks and residential neighborhoods
key_sites.description: Common backyard bird throughout Seattle metro; uses feeders, nest boxes, ornamental plantings

key_sites.name: Discovery Park
key_sites.description: Mixed woodland with mature conifers and deciduous edge

key_sites.name: Washington Park Arboretum
key_sites.description: Diverse woodland plantings supporting dense chickadee populations

key_sites.name: Seward Park
key_sites.description: Old-growth remnant with mixed-species flocks including chickadee as nuclear species
